snippets.title: Anatomy: base case + recursive case
snippets.explanation: Without the base case the function would call itself forever; without strict progress (`n - 1`) it would never reach the base case.

snippets.title: Tree traversal (yield-based)
snippets.explanation: Generators + recursion give you a clean traversal API; no need to materialize the whole list.

snippets.title: Memoize to collapse overlapping sub-problems
snippets.explanation: lru_cache turns O(2ⁿ) into O(n) by remembering each sub-result the first time it's computed.

snippets.title: Iterative version of a tree DFS
snippets.code:
def walk_iter(root):
    stack = [root]
    while stack:
        node = stack.pop()
        if node is None: continue
        yield node.value
        # push children in reverse so the leftmost is processed first
        stack.extend(reversed(node.children))

# Same Big-O as recursion (O(V+E)), no Python recursion-depth limit.
snippets.explanation: When you need to walk a large or untrusted tree, simulate the call stack with a list and loop.

snippets.title: Tail-recursion → loop (Python has no TCO)
snippets.explanation: Some languages collapse the tail call into a loop automatically. Python doesn't, so do it yourself when n can be large.

snippets.title: Raise the recursion limit (last-resort escape hatch)
snippets.code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(100_000)   # default ~1000

# Use sparingly — deep recursion still costs C-stack memory and can crash the interpreter.
snippets.explanation: Raising the limit is a quick fix for medium-deep cases; for truly deep recursion convert to iteration.
